frames: The agent is stationary, low to the ground, with all four legs spread and in contact.
The agent slightly lifts its front-left leg, initiating a subtle weight shift.
The front-left leg moves forward while the body remains low, maintaining three-point contact.
The front-left leg touches down, and the agent's body slightly adjusts its low posture.
The agent lifts its front-right leg, extending it forward while keeping its body low.
The front-right leg makes contact with the ground, and the agent's body remains in a low stance.
The agent lifts its back-left leg, moving it forward while the body stays low and stable.
The back-left leg touches down, and the agent settles into a low, stable, four-legged posture.
